title: 1-hour extended prompt-cache TTL for the stable Morty persona prefix
source: Anthropic prompt caching docs (cache_control ttl:"1h" extended cache) — docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching; live fetch blocked this run
description: delegate.py already tracks cache_read/cache_write_5m pricing but relies on the default 5-minute cache TTL. Within a single sprint, dozens of Morty subprocesses reuse an identical persona/system prefix over a span far longer than 5 minutes, so the cache repeatedly expires and pays the write premium again. Anthropic's 1-hour cache TTL keeps the stable prefix hot across an entire sprint, cutting cache-write costs substantially.
proposed_changes: Split each agent prompt into a stable prefix (persona + role rules + effort guidance from persona.py) and a volatile suffix (ticket body, file context). Ensure the stable prefix is byte-identical across Morty runs in a sprint. When invoking via the Anthropic SDK path, set cache_control {type:'ephemeral', ttl:'1h'} on the last block of the prefix; for the `claude -p` path, order the prompt so the cacheable prefix comes first and document the 1h-cache assumption. Add a 'cache_write_1h' pricing row (input * 2.0) to the PRICING table and surface 1h vs 5m savings in the cost breakdown.
